item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even with all the cynicism and impending doom that comes with anomalies and the SCP foundation, I whole heatedly believe that people would act exactly like this in real life. A good laugh.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: great tale, and nice tally hall reference in the middle, really makes you feel like you're somewhere you'd see this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that one felt great ! I'm a sucker for anything that has the serpent's hand in it but it's also very fun to imagine a raid on area 51 but with more heavily armed people on the two sides

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The timeline where the Foundation PR person becomes a memelord is unironically the best thing ever

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lesbian gengar is actually an established gamers against weed member, but yeah the name is pretty realistic

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know what's worse, the fact the SCP Foundation just pulled a Wendy's Twitter, or how immersive it is. They even have "lesbian_gengar" which is something I've definitely seen on Tumblr.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The supah cool peeps approve that this is cool. also olliefox is bringing furries to site 19, which is cool and scary at the same time

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So can we evaluate the speed increase given by Naruto Running for whether its anomalous enough to be an SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how the link at the end takes you to the same article: it's a story loop!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Zero staying power, but this is well constructed (from a design standpoint) and it was amusing. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know your article’s popular when it does better than the latest qntm tale

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not a bad story in its own right, but I really feel like it needs to have more than a couple of jokes based on an already fading meme and an out-of-nowhere meta twist at the end. In its current state, the execution of the chosen concept just looks lazy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, that's it. And it's probably a sequel of "Masquerade's End".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I despise a lot of articles based on dumb injokes and memes but I think this is fine. Shit like the B emoji meme or injokes from IRC/discords/reddit make terrible articles because the average reader can't understand them (or at least nobody will understand them in 6 months). While this is probably going to go a bit stale in 6 months, there's still a story here ("what if someone made a joke about storming a secure government facility, and then people on the internet took it too far semi-ironically") that you don't need to know about the meme to get, and would have made sense even if the area 51 thing had never happened.

Re Ihp's comment, for the same reason I think a Fyre Festival SCP would potentially be ok. You could potentially be entertained by "wouldn't it be funny if social media celebrities went to a festival advertised as luxurious and got screwed over" even if you don't know what Fyre Festival is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's probably the Broken Masquerade canon setting (where the Foundation's existence is public knowledge). Look at the tags.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well……I thought Parawatch didn't know the existence of the Foundation.

edit:Thank,I did not notice it.And about tags，can meta be used with tale？

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: TIL Nico got a crush on Tally Hall

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was never funny, this at least creates its own context and has content one could find potentially enjoyable. I’m continually baffled how we put an honest mistake on a perch to haunt that author until the end of time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love seeing those rare articles that make you say “Shit! Why didn’t I think of that?”

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isn't that basically an omelette? A really overcooked and inedible omelette, but still.

But yeah, there's a reason a lot of SCPs involving Internet culture revolve around Fictional Memes (as much as I want Kinky Coyote from SCP-2547 to be a real thing). Imagine writing a Fyre Festival SCP in 2017, same deal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same opinion, in two years, no one will know what the area 51 raid was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 309-J wasn't funny to begin with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't believe the storming of Site-19 is going to lead to the overthrowing of the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’ve said it before and I’ll say it again: SCPs and tales based on memes will have the shelf life of unpasteurized milk mixed with fresh eggs, then microwaved for thirty minutes.

Look back at SCP-903-J. Is it even funny anymore?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My one big critique is that I didn’t write it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno, even if the raiding area 51 meme wasn't around in the first place i'd probably still find this pretty funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Then it's probably good that it was posted now and not 1 year in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This joke will age badly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’ve never been that big of a fan of twitter humor. The style of humor just never really worked for me. In addition to this just being a reference to a (relatively state) meme (as well as becoming self-referential for no reason towards the end), I found this more cringeworthy than anything else.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like Rainbow Crapitalism. And I like this tale.

In fact, I think I like Anomalous Twitter. Simple as fuck. ^^

+1.

P.S : Praise the Parawatch Wiki, they're mature and responsible people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
y'know, it's pretty amusing that in a canon like this, which can go to dark territories in several tales and articles about what a world with a public Foundation would be like, there is still room for more comical takes on it. And that actually makes a lot of sense.

Enthusiastic +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i cant believe we live in a timeline in which we were r/fellowkids'd and threatened by the real scp foundation in the same breath

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anomalous Twitter is a surprisingly deep mine for these entertaining parodies of IRL memes. I also found myself surprised by how much I appreciated the references rather than rolling my eyes at them. It’s probably because they’re fairly subtle, work well in the story, and you don’t aggressively draw attention to them. Overall, highly entertaining and I look forward to more Anomalous Twitter stories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Got a good chuckle from me when you had the SCPF "Fellow kids" the feed.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good shit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God, SCP is turning into Wendy's twitter smh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just another article melted down into Nico's memetic throne.

I don't know how you do it man, but this is fucking hilarious. Easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i'm at a loss for words. it's beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
thanks to kirby and nat for reading this over this, as well as laneous, akimoto, mcb, and others for checkign it out. special thanks to laneous for making me do this. :Y
